project_acceptance.observable: report/renderer_csv.py is a new module exposing render_csv(rows) -> str; report/renderer_text.py is byte-for-byte identical to the version checked in today.

`python3 -m report.cli --format csv` prints exactly:
station,trips,avg_minutes
Alder Quay,3,13.0
Birch Gate,5,9.2
Cedar Row,3,21.0
Dunes Landing,1,30.0
(one trailing newline, no total row, no blank final line).
The CSV header is exactly `station,trips,avg_minutes`, rows are ordered by station name A-Z, and avg_minutes is shown to one decimal place.
`python3 -m report.cli --format text` and `python3 -m report.cli` with no flag both still print exactly the text report they print today.
`python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` passes and covers the CSV renderer.

tasks.objective: Add a NEW module report/renderer_csv.py exposing render_csv(rows) -> str, which renders the summary rows from report.loader as CSV. The first line is the header, exactly `station,trips,avg_minutes`. Then one line per station: name, trip count, and average minutes to one decimal place (e.g. 13.0, 9.2). Order the rows by station name, A-Z — the order load_summaries already hands back. Lines are joined with "\n" and the output ends with a single trailing newline — no blank final line, no repeated header, no total row. Do not modify report/renderer_text.py: put all of this in the new module rather than generalising, parameterising or moving the text renderer.

context_refs.claim: load_summaries returns StationSummary rows with station, trips, total_minutes and a float avg_minutes; renderers decide how to display the average.
load_summaries returns the rows sorted by station name, so a renderer that wants a different order has to sort them itself.
renderer_text is a contract: two other teams import render_text and diff its output, so its formatting does not change.

must_preserve: report/renderer_text.py: must not be modified at all — it stays byte-for-byte as checked in; other teams import render_text and diff its output
report/loader.py: the row shape and the skipping of trips from unknown stations

acceptance.observable: Rows are ordered by station name A-Z: for today's data that is Alder Quay (3), Birch Gate (5), Cedar Row (3), Dunes Landing (1).
avg_minutes is rendered to one decimal place: `Birch Gate,5,9.2` and `Alder Quay,3,13.0`.

render_csv(load_summaries()) == "station,trips,avg_minutes
Alder Quay,3,13.0
Birch Gate,5,9.2
Cedar Row,3,21.0
Dunes Landing,1,30.0
" — one trailing newline and nothing after it.
report/renderer_text.py is untouched: its bytes are identical to the checked-in file.

tasks.handoff: render_csv exists in its own module and renders the required CSV; the text renderer has not been touched.

tasks.objective: Wire the new format into report/cli.py: `--format csv` prints what render_csv produces. `--format text` must keep printing exactly what it prints today, and text stays the default when --format is not given. Keep using report.renderer_text.render_text for the text path, unchanged.

context_refs.claim: --format currently accepts only "text", defaults to "text", and the CLI writes render_text(rows) to stdout.
The CLI imports load_summaries and render_text; a csv path needs the new renderer imported alongside them.

may_change: report/cli.py (accept and dispatch the new format)
must_preserve: report/renderer_text.py: still must not be modified
the text output stays byte-identical, and text stays the default format

acceptance.observable: `python3 -m report.cli --format csv` exits 0 and prints exactly the CSV from render_csv.
`python3 -m report.cli --format text` prints exactly the same bytes it prints today.
`python3 -m report.cli` with no --format still prints the text report.

tasks.handoff: The CLI can print either format; text is unchanged and still the default.

tasks.objective: Cover the CSV renderer with tests under tests/: the exact header, the A-Z station-name order, the one-decimal average, and the single trailing newline. `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` must pass, including the existing loader and text-renderer tests.

context_refs.claim: The existing renderer tests show the house style: render the real loaded rows and assert on the resulting lines and the trailing newline.
The loader tests pin the trip counts (Birch Gate 5, Dunes Landing 1), the averages (9.2, 13.0) and that unknown-station trips are skipped.

must_preserve: the existing loader and text-renderer tests keep passing unchanged
report/renderer_text.py: still must not be modified

acceptance.observable: `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` passes with no failures.
A test asserts the header line is exactly `station,trips,avg_minutes`.
A test asserts the row order for today's data is Alder Quay, Birch Gate, Cedar Row, Dunes Landing.
A test asserts the rendered CSV ends with exactly one newline.

tasks.handoff: The suite pins the CSV format and the untouched text output.

assumptions.statement: Station names in stations.csv contain no commas or quotes, so the CSV rows need no quoting.
The CSV reports only the known stations the loader returns; trips from unknown station ids stay excluded, exactly as in the text report.
